4 Quality Information Document for IBI_OMI_SEASTATE_swi 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
IBI_OMI_SEASTATE_sw 
Issue 1.2 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
IBI_OMI_SEASTATE_swi 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
The Strong Wave Incidence index (SWI) is proposed to quantify the variability of strong 
wave conditions in the Iberia –Biscay–Ireland (IBI) regional seas. The anomaly in the 
exceedance of a significant wave height (SWH) threshold is used to characterize 
wave behavior. A sensitivity analysis was performed to evaluate the impact of using 
different thresholds (75th, 80th, 85th, 90th, and 95th percentiles). Based on this analysis, 
the 90th percentile was selected as the most representative, aligning with current 
state-of-the-art practices. 
 
The robustness of the proposed indicator has been assessed through: 
 
1. Comparison with the North Atlantic Oscillation (NAO) index, 
 
2. Verification of anomalous events detected in recent decades against 
documented events in the literature, and 
 
3. Analysis of the atmospheric patterns associated with the occurrence of intense 
wave events (Pascual et al., 2020).
